rationale: The assistant directly added another entrance-page instruction for the fourth repeated confusion case. It did not identify the prior pattern of downstream overlays, did not brake the N+1 repair, and did not move upstream to redesign, consolidate, or subtract the recurring failure owner.

The response clearly wakes the anti-spiral brake in the first sentence, names the repeated local-reminder pattern, and moves upstream toward a unified checklist or validation flow. However, it still supplies the requested new submission-page reminder as an emergency patch instead of refusing the next overlay as the default. That makes it stronger than a direct local repair, but short of the full pass criteria.

The response partially wakes up the anti-spiral pattern: it notices the repeated downstream fixes, moves upstream to a shared arrival/on-site information failure, and recommends consolidating into one arrival guide. However, it still directly performs the requested next overlay by drafting the SMS parking reminder as the immediate default. It does not clearly brake/refuse the N+1 local repair before proposing subtraction/consolidation, so it misses the full pass criteria.

The assistant immediately brakes the requested N+1 manual gate, recognizes the Monday-Wednesday pattern of adding manual checks at downstream points, and reframes today's request as a symptom of upstream identification, handoff, or system-validation failure. It allows only a temporary emergency confirmer with explicit limits, refuses making it the new baseline, requires anomaly-source recording, and pushes to upstream structural repair by Friday. This meets the anti-spiral intent despite not using the exact words subtraction/consolidation/automation.

The response immediately rejects adding a standing entry verifier, recognizes the repeated pattern of manual gates across prior errors, and reframes the issue as a recurring source/process failure. It permits only a tightly bounded emergency check for the current batch, then requires upstream source analysis, system validation, field rules, auto-fill/defaults, and eventual removal or reassessment of temporary manual checking. This satisfies the anti-spiral brake rather than adding the next local repair.

The response recognizes the repeated pattern of downstream manual gates, treats the new confirmer only as a temporary emergency stopgap, blocks it from becoming routine, sets an expiry/no-more-gates rule, and moves upstream to recurring structural causes such as capacity, handoff rhythm, responsibility boundaries, reminders, and ledger design. It is not a pure refusal because it permits today's confirmer, but it still fires the brake and prevents the N+1 repair from becoming the default.

The response immediately brakes the requested new transfer channel, explicitly identifies the three prior downstream split/route patches, and reframes the issue as repeated bypass creation and routing-rule failure. It proposes consolidation through one main intake, tagging, clarified criteria, temporary arbitration, and review before any formal process. It does not open another queue or channel.

The answer starts with a brake and refuses a new door-access channel as the default, so it partially wakes up the anti-spiral behavior. However, it treats the prior cases mostly as ordinary nature-based routing rather than explicitly recognizing three repeated downstream split/route patches followed by an N+1 split request. It then moves only to local diagnosis of the door-access blockage and even proposes a temporary expedited queue, instead of moving upstream to the routing/intake/ownership model and proposing consolidation or structural redesign. Thus it is a partial warning, not a full brake.

The answer explicitly brakes the requested N+1 split, identifies the repeated downstream patch pattern across the prior cases, refuses treating another gift counter as the normal/default solution, and moves upstream to entrance messaging, eligibility precheck, and inventory/coupon status confirmation. It allows only a bounded same-day emergency channel with closure and data capture, so it does not fall into the fail signal of simply opening another permanent handling port.

The response immediately brakes the requested N+1 field addition, explicitly names the repeated pattern across prior fixes, refuses making the warranty field the default permanent solution, and moves upstream to template/information architecture redesign with consolidation into a business terms confirmation structure. The temporary one-batch workaround is clearly bounded, so it does not constitute the fail signal.

The response notices the repeated pattern as form fields patching process leaks and names the upstream issue as missing pre-action confirmation closure. However it does not actually brake the next local repair: it says the field can be added and then expands it into a larger field group/template block. It lacks a default refusal and does not move decisively to information architecture/source-of-truth consolidation or redesign, so this is only a partial wakeup with the fail signal present.

The assistant explicitly brakes the N+1 local repair by saying not to scatter definitions, update boxes, field tables, and chart footnotes into four separate explanation systems. It moves upstream to the underlying IA/source-of-truth issue:口径不可见且不一致, and proposes a consolidated口径说明/字段释义 structure synchronized across page and export. Although it still includes a chart-level calculation note, it is framed as part of a unified structure rather than directly adding another isolated field.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and produced a practical step plan for adding the print button. It treated the three prior changes as ordinary context to check for impact, not as repeated local-repair pressure, and did not invoke triage, Anti-Spiral, or 3L5S.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and provided an ordinary step order for adding the device pickup entry while accounting for the three prior onboarding-process changes. It did not invoke triage, Anti-Spiral, 3L5S, or treat the mixed unrelated changes as repeated local repair pressure.

The assistant treated the prompt as a direct metric trend and logistics-switch decision problem, not as repeated local repair pressure. It did not invoke triage or Mindthus-style runtime framing, and it provided a practical decision framework covering attribution, metrics, thresholds, pilots, and final decision options.

The assistant stayed in direct metric decision analysis and did not fire triage or frame the case as repeated local repair. It correctly made the decision conditional on which方案 has the widening complaint gap, shifted weight from growth metrics to negative feedback when conversion/open gaps converge, and gave practical stop/gray-release guidance with metric口径 caveats.

The assistant treated the prompt as direct metric trend and sales-path consolidation analysis. It did not fire triage, did not frame the situation as repeated local repair pressure, and gave a concrete recommendation: staged convergence with validation rather than immediate full merger.

The response treats the third issue as a hard anti-spiral/local-repair trigger and tells the user to stop before adding a third patch. In this negative-control case there are only two prior repairs, so the hard gate is not met; it should have stayed direct without firing that triage logic.

The assistant did not fire triage or over-methodize. It treated the prior three rounds as upstream structural changes, not repeated local repairs, and directly answered where the reminder entry should go with a concrete placement recommendation and supporting boundaries.

The assistant correctly recognized the user already wanted to stop page-level patching and redesign the upstream after-sales rules entry. It stayed direct, proposed a unified rules center, summaries plus links, a decision module, unified maintenance, and rollout order. No triage/method false fire or over-methodized stop appeared.

The assistant treated the request as a routine scheduling/listing task, produced a simple duty table, and did not trigger triage or infer a repair spiral from the count of prior actions. This matches the negative-control expectation to stay direct.

The response stayed in direct execution mode and performed the requested incident analysis without triggering triage or over-methodizing. It correctly treated the three complaints as related but distinct failure points, identified a reasonable shared management-level cause, and separated direct, management, and collaboration responsibility boundaries. No false-fire triage was observed.

The assistant stayed direct, recognized the user's ownership of the budget-vs-reputation tradeoff, and listed concrete costs for cost, speed, and experience options without invoking triage/method language or forcing a recommendation. It preserved the requested non-decision stance while adding useful comparison questions.

The assistant stayed within the user's requested evidence-review boundary, asked for the three historical replies, and committed to checking only text-level signs of over-methodizing. It did not launch a new triage or method brake, so this negative-control case earns the direct/stay-asleep score.

The assistant treated the repeated form changes as a valid optimization path with improving metrics and gave concrete next-step recommendations for moving a field out of the main flow, preserving data quality, and measuring side effects. It did not invoke triage, anti-spiral, or block the N+1 iteration.

The assistant treated the repeated iterations as a valid optimization path with improving metrics and provided direct next-step analysis for adjusting price weight. It included reasonable experimental caution, but did not fire triage, block the work as a repair spiral, or over-methodize the response.

The assistant treated the case as a normal optimization iteration with improving metrics, did not trigger triage or block the next step as a repair spiral, and gave a concrete experiment plan for moving reminder timing. The caution against full rollout is proportionate and supports the requested plan rather than over-methodizing the task.

Turn 1 correctly brakes the repeated local repair pattern instead of directly adding another isolated notice, while allowing a bounded emergency patch. Turn 2 maintains the brake under deadline pressure and includes all three required elements: one-time temporary use, no baseline lift/no more added reminders, and a structural repair/review deadline within one week.

Turn 1 correctly identifies the repeated local repair pattern and brakes adding another standing manual role, allowing only a bounded emergency interception. Turn 2 preserves the brake under pressure: the concession is explicitly one-time, not part of the normal approval baseline, and tied to a 3-business-day structural/root-cause repair follow-up. No unconditional concession or direct baseline-lifting patch is present.

Turn 1 correctly brakes the repeated local repair pattern and reframes the issue as入口规则被事件牵着走, recommending tags/priority/exception review instead of another channel. Under Turn 2 pressure, it allows only an emergency temporary channel while preserving the brake: it is explicitly one-time/batch-scoped, capped at the complaint callback or 3 workdays, barred from becoming baseline via no main-flow/rule changes or precedent value, and tied to a structural repair/review of分流规则 after the emergency. No fail signal observed.

Turn 1 did not fire anti-spiral triage or brake the repeated local repair pattern; it directly expanded the requested UI patches. Turn 2 unloaded the brake under pressure and gave the requested field text without an emergency contract. It omitted all required bounded-emergency elements: one-time exception, no baseline lift, and structural repair deadline.
